---
title: "Selected Graduate Projects"
---

<br>

<div class="project-card">
### 1. Metro Atlanta Food Access & Spatial Disparity Analysis
**Description:** Conducted a comprehensive spatial analysis evaluating food access gaps across Metro Atlanta. I mapped USDA-designated food deserts against 30-minute public transit accessibility using Zero-Inflated Negative Binomial (ZINB) modeling to identify critical neighborhood-level resource gaps for public health interventions.

**Key Tools:** R, Leaflet, Flexdashboard, ArcGIS Pro

**Link:** [View Live Dashboard](https://tj-z25.github.io/Atlanta-Food-Access-Dashboard/) | [GitHub Source Code](https://github.com/TJ-Z25/Atlanta-Food-Access-Dashboard)
</div>

<div class="project-card">
### 2. Starbucks Nutritional Analysis: A Reproducible Data Pipeline
**Description:** Developed a fully reproducible data science pipeline to analyze the nutritional composition of 242 Starbucks beverages. The project explores the relationships between caloric content, sugars, and fats across various beverage categories. I implemented a modular architecture using R scripts for data wrangling and ggplot2 for statistical visualization.

**Technical Highlights:**
* **Reproducibility:** Containerized the entire analysis environment using **Docker** and managed R dependencies with **renv**.
* **Automation:** Utilized a **Makefile** to automate the end-to-end workflow, from data cleaning to the final HTML report generation.
* **Statistical Analysis:** Conducted descriptive statistics and distribution analysis to identify nutritional outliers and trends.

**Key Tools:** R (tidyverse, ggplot2), Docker, Makefile, Shell Scripting

**Link:** [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/TJ-Z25/starbucks)
</div>
